claim: Prediction in atom ATOM_T011_S016_PRE_01 ("And so if you train a neural net to make a single prediction for what's going...") holds if and only if the first-principle constraint in atom ATOM_T013_S025_FIR_01 ("But we are not losing the sight of the fact that it is in principle possible ...") is binding.
first_principles_validity_hypothesis: The prediction "And so if you train a neural net to make a single predict..." is forced by the underlying constraint "But we are not losing the sight of the fact that it is in..."; if the constraint is not binding the prediction may still hold by accident but the binding case is the load-bearing version of this candidate.
why_novel_vs_speaker: The speaker introduces the prediction (snippet S016) and the first-principle (snippet S025) in separate contexts; the speaker does not state that the second forces the first.
why_potentially_useful: A prediction that is forced by a first-principle is more falsifiable than a free-floating prediction: if the principle holds and the prediction fails, one of them is wrong, which is a binary research question.
